text: The video begins with a view of the left chest anatomy, highlighting the aortic arch. The pulmonary vessels are visible, including the superior and inferior pulmonary veins. The pulmonary ligament is being divided to expose the inferior pulmonary vein during dissection in the hilum.
The surgeon has divided the fissure, revealing the branches of the pulmonary artery that supply the upper lobe and lingula. As the lung is lifted, the pericardium and phrenic nerve become visible.
The video shows the internal mammary vessels and the sympathetic chain near the thoracic inlet, with the subclavian vessel also visible.
